name: Heavy dark matter decay (PeV neutrino source)
summary: Superheavy dark matter (m_DM ~ 10^6 - 10^10 GeV) decaying with lifetime tau_DM ~ 10^28 s through dimension-six SM-suppressed operators (e.g., L L H H or L H sigma_mu nu) produces a diffuse high-energy neutrino flux that can account for a significant fraction of IceCube's observed astrophysical neutrino background. The decay channel chi -> nu nu produces a hard line plus cascade neutrinos; decay through L L H H produces a softer spectrum. Distinguishable from astrophysical sources by isotropy and from heavy-DM annihilation by the lifetime scaling (annihilation requires extremely small sigma v).

addresses_puzzles.note: Lifetime constraints from IceCube cascade-data analyses: tau_DM > 10^28 s for m_DM ~ 100 TeV - 10 PeV through visible channels. A subdominant DM-decay contribution (~10-30% of diffuse flux) is allowed; full attribution requires fine-tuned spectral fit. Bhattacharya et al. (2019, arXiv:1903.12623) and Esmaili-Serpico (2013, arXiv:1308.1105) provide updated bounds.

Superheavy DM produced via gravitational pair-production at the end of inflation (Kolb-Long 1998) or through curvaton-like inflaton couplings. Doesn't require thermal equilibrium; relic abundance set by post-inflationary entropy dilution.

new_fields: superheavy fermionic or scalar dark matter chi
new_symmetries: approximate Z_2 stabilisation, broken at dimension >= 5
introduces.note: DM mass, operator coefficient (sets lifetime), branching fractions to nu/charged-lepton/hadron channels.

history: Berezinsky, Kachelriess, Vilenkin (1997), 'Ultrahigh energy cosmic rays without GZK cutoff', PRL 79, 4302 - first DM-decay UHECR proposal. IceCube-era reincarnation: Feldstein-Kusenko-Matsumoto-Yanagida (2013, arXiv:1303.7320), Esmaili-Serpico (2013, arXiv:1308.1105). Updated post-IceCube-10yr: Bhattacharya, Esmaili, Palomares-Ruiz, Sarcevic (2019, arXiv:1903.12623).

references.citation: Esmaili and Serpico, 'Are IceCube neutrinos unveiling PeV-scale decaying dark matter?', JCAP 11, 054 (2013)
Berezinsky, Kachelriess, Vilenkin, 'Ultrahigh energy cosmic rays without GZK cutoff', Phys. Rev. Lett. 79, 4302 (1997)
